published_prior_art.cite: DingLu2017 and JiangYangDing2022: principal-score formulas identify principal causal effects under principal ignorability
NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024: two-stratum PI-violation sensitivity; DornGuoKallus2022: sharp odds-ratio bounds for unmeasured confounding

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers: global ordinal response-path bounds can be strictly tighter than pairwise adjacent binary relaxations

published_prior_art.cite: WuAntonelli2024 and TongKahanHarhayLi2025 cover PI-violation partial ID / odds-ratio sensitivity, but not a finite ordinal adjacent posterior-chain sharpness theorem

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The strict binary-collapse failure targets B_bin, but no cited paper or estimator actually uses the edgewise adjacent-binary-copy relaxation for ordinal intermediates; tier=letter below novelty_target=field until a real published target is named.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: WangZhangMealliBornkamp2023 is a binary discontinuation/intercurrent-event MI sensitivity workflow, not an ordinal edgewise binary-collapse method with shared middle strata.

soundness_flags.label: Setup / Main results
soundness_flags.one_line: The endpoint notation Q(P,Gamma), L_g(P,Gamma), U_g(P,Gamma), and B_bin(P,Gamma;g) omits the known principal-score vector pi, although pi is not determined by the observed law P.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: B_bin is not defined as an exact optimization problem when the target stratum has multiple incident edge copies; the text says it is evaluated over every copy but does not specify the lower/upper aggregation rule.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the generic binary-collapse target with a named published ordinal dichotomization or adjacent-binary workflow, or explicitly downgrade the strict-gap clause to a methodological warning rather than a refutation.
Promote pi into the formal arguments and locked parameters for Q, L/U, and B_bin, e.g. L_g(P,pi,Gamma), so two laws with the same observed P but different principal scores are not conflated.
Define B_bin as a precise LP relaxation with variables, constraints, objective, and endpoint aggregation for middle-stratum targets; then state the strict inclusion against that LP.

Add a calibration result unique to posterior principal-score divergence, such as a dual characterization or a boundary case that cannot be read as the same global-versus-pairwise compatibility pattern from pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to make pi and B_bin mathematically explicit and either name a real published ordinal binary-collapse target or soften the strict-gap claim below field-tier ambition.
